steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The phrase "to end a debate" shows that the missing word describes what senators could do with a measure to stop prolonged discussion.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
The blank describes what could be done to a formal measure to end a prolonged debate. Substitute each word into "no formal measure...that could be ______ to end a debate":

A. Improvised means made up on the spot without preparation. The passage is about a formal, established procedure — improvised is the opposite of what a formal Senate measure is.

B. Invoked means to call upon or put an official rule into effect. "A measure that could be invoked to end a debate" — senators formally activating a procedure to stop deliberation. This matches the procedural, parliamentary context. ✓

C. Abolished means to formally end or eliminate something permanently. Abolishing a measure would destroy it entirely; the Senate needed to use a measure, not get rid of one.

D. Partitioned means to divide into separate parts. Partitioning a measure makes no sense in the context of using a procedure to curtail debate.

B is correct.
